name: De'Longhi HMP1500 Mica Panel Heater
tag: Best Flat Panel Heater for Bedrooms
introText: The De'Longhi HMP1500 is a silent mica panel heater with no fan, digital thermostat, anti-freeze mode, and wall-mount option — the right choice for bedrooms where fan noise from ceramic heaters is a problem.

specs.Noise: Silent — no fan, no moving parts

whatWorks: Truly silent operation — mica panel heaters produce heat through natural convection (no fan), creating zero noise. This is the primary reason to choose a panel heater over ceramic fan heaters: complete silence makes it ideal for bedrooms, nurseries, and offices during calls.

Precise digital thermostat maintains a set temperature without manual adjustment — set it to 20°C and it cycles on and off to maintain that temperature, more accurately than dial-based thermostats on cheaper units.

Wall-mount option maximizes floor space and keeps the heater permanently out of the way — useful in bedrooms with limited floor area, and eliminates the tip-over risk associated with free-standing heaters.
5°C anti-freeze mode runs continuously at minimum power to prevent pipes from freezing in cottages or unheated spaces — practical for seasonal property protection.
worthKnowing: Panel heaters heat slowly compared to ceramic fan heaters — the absence of a fan means heated air rises naturally rather than being projected into the room. Cold-room warm-up time is significantly longer than a Lasko 755320 or Vornado MVH.
Best as a temperature-maintenance heater, not a fast-heat heater — effective strategy is to set it and leave it running rather than using it to quickly warm a cold room.

1500W is the maximum power draw of any standard 120V household heater — all electric heaters, regardless of design, are capped by the circuit amperage. De'Longhi's advantage is delivery and control, not wattage.

Radiant heat feels different from forced-air heat — mica panel heat warms objects and people directly (like sunlight) rather than warming air. Some people find radiant heat more comfortable; others prefer the faster warm-up of forced air.

commonComplaints.title: Heats rooms slowly without a fan…
commonComplaints.body: Heats rooms slowly without a fan — radiant panel heaters rely on natural convection, meaning cold rooms take significantly longer to warm up than ceramic fan heaters at the same wattage.

commonComplaints.title: Not suitable for fast warm-up in…
commonComplaints.body: Not suitable for fast warm-up in very cold rooms — if you need to quickly heat a room that's at 10°C, a ceramic fan heater like the Vornado MVH will do it faster.

commonComplaints.title: Wall mounting requires drilling and may…
commonComplaints.body: Wall mounting requires drilling and may not suit renters — permanent wall installation isn't possible for all living situations, limiting the space-saving mounting benefit.

verdict: The De'Longhi HMP1500 is the right heater for bedrooms and noise-sensitive spaces where silence matters more than fast heat. If you run a heater overnight and want zero noise, the panel design is unmatched. If you need to quickly heat a cold garage, workshop, or large room, a ceramic fan heater like the Vornado MVH or Lasko 755320 is more effective.

realBuyers.quote: I can sleep without waking up to the fan click on and off all night. Completely silent. This was the only reason I switched from my Lasko and it was the right call.
The digital thermostat is great. Set it to 20° and it holds there accurately all night. No more waking up sweating or freezing.
Perfect for my home office during calls. No fan noise bleeding into meetings. Works exactly as described.

sources.title: Panel Heater vs Fan Heater — Reddit (r/homeimprovement)

metaDescription: De'Longhi HMP1500 review for Canada: 1500W mica panel, silent operation, digital thermostat, wall-mountable, anti-freeze mode. ~$149 CAD on Amazon.ca.
